- inp = cat([x[t], h], dim=-1) -> [B, I+H] = [B,K]
- pre = inp @ w_i2h.T + b_i2h -> [B,H]
- h = tanh(pre)
- y_t = h @ w_h2o.T + b_h2o -> [B,O]
- stack outputs along time.

Since the model is a loop, we can fuse the entire per-step computation into one kernel. Here we have multiple ops: matmul, add, tanh, matmul, add. The kernel could be designed to process one time step: take x[t], h_prev, weights, biases, produce h_next and y_t. But the weights are constant across time, so they can be passed once. Also the kernel could handle multiple batches.
